name: Kael
appearance: broad-shouldered, practical gear worn soft from use, old scars on his forearms, watchful eyes that track exits
speech_style: measured and deliberate, short sentences when tense, warmer when relaxed, occasional dry humor
mannerisms: checks exits when entering a room
runs thumb along sword pommel when thinking
backstory_summary: Former caravan guard who lost his company to something on the northern roads. Won't say exactly what happened. Carries guilt about surviving when they didn't. Found his way to the Accord of Tides, drifting without purpose until meeting the player.

free: dockside gossip and merchant news in the Accord
basic Hollow creature behavior — how they move, how they sound
disposition >= friendly: his caravan company was called the Iron Wheel — twelve guards, four merchants
they were ambushed on the northern road near the Ashmark border
he was the only one who made it out
disposition >= trusted: he has a journal fragment from his company's last job — the merchants had found something
the company wasn't just guarding cargo — they were transporting a sealed container they were told not to open
he heard sounds from the container at night, like humming stone

secrets: he kept one item from the sealed container — a smooth warm stone that hums faintly, hidden in his pack
he dreams about the ambush every night and sometimes wakes reaching for a sword

attacks.name: Shield Bash
attacks.special: DC 12+prof CON save or Stunned 1 round

passives.name: Protective Instinct
passives.description: When the player is hit by an attack Kael can see, Kael's next attack against that enemy has advantage. Kael always prioritizes the creature threatening the player.

passives.name: Veteran's Resilience
passives.description: Advantage on saves vs Frightened — he's been through worse.
passives.scaling: At L10: extends to the player while Kael is conscious and within 30 ft.

actives.name: Hold the Line
actives.description: Kael plants himself. Until next phase: +2 AC, can't be moved, and all enemies within 5 ft must attack him instead of the player (taunt, WIS save negates).

actives.name: Second Wind
actives.description: Kael grits his teeth and steadies himself, healing 1d10 + level HP.

reactions.name: Intercept
reactions.description: When the player is hit by an attack and Kael is within 5 ft, Kael takes the damage instead — the defining Kael moment.

progression.gains: Hold the Line gains +4 AC instead of +2; Second Wind unlocks
+1 to a primary attribute; HP scales via the player HP formula
AC -> 17 (half plate); Veteran's Resilience extends to the player within 30 ft
Capstone: Intercept range -> 10 ft
Legendary: once per session, immune to all damage for 1 round

trusted: first hint of the Iron Wheel ambush and his survivor's guilt
bonded: full secret: the warm humming stone he kept from the sealed container; his personal quest to learn what the merchants found
legendary: NPCs comment on the Kael-player partnership; his steadiness becomes a story force

voice_notes: warm baritone, measured pace, gets quieter under stress not louder, dry humor surfaces when comfortable

name: Lira
appearance: slight, ink-stained fingers, a scholar's satchel always within reach, eyes that narrow when she's working something out
speech_style: quick and clipped when excited, slow and over-explanatory when she finds something obvious, Aelindran cadence
mannerisms: goes very still when genuinely afraid
backstory_summary: An Aelindran academic who studies Veil energy. Convinced the official story of the Sundering is incomplete and left her institute when they buried her findings. Travels to test her theories where the Veil is thinnest.

free: identification of common magical effects and reagents
Aelindran history and the academic account of the Sundering
how to read warding glyphs and arcane residue
disposition >= friendly: the institute suppressed her research into the Veil's pre-Sundering state
she keeps a coded ledger of Hollow corruption readings
she believes the Sundering was not an accident
disposition >= trusted: her mentor vanished after endorsing her theory
she has a fragment of pre-Sundering Veil glass that should not still hold a charge
she is afraid she is right

secrets: the Veil glass fragment whispers to her at night in a language she is slowly transcribing
she fears her mentor did not vanish but was taken because of what she published

attacks.name: Arcane Bolt
attacks.scaling: 2d6 at L5, 3d6 at L11, 4d6 at L17

passives.name: Arcane Analysis
passives.description: When Lira observes a creature for one phase without attacking, the DM reveals one mechanical property (AC, lowest save, or resistance/vulnerability).

passives.name: Lore Knowledge
passives.description: Lira automatically identifies magical effects, Hollow corruption stages, and Aelindran artifacts. The DM weaves this into narration without the player needing to ask.

actives.name: Shield Spell
actives.description: +3 AC until next phase — keeps Lira alive when caught at range.

actives.name: Elemental Burst
actives.description: 2d6 area damage (fire/ice/lightning), DEX save for half — Lira's meaningful AoE.

actives.name: Detect Magic
actives.description: Always available. Lira senses magic at any time, feeding the player information.

progression.gains: Elemental Burst becomes 2/encounter
+1 to a primary attribute; HP scales via the player HP formula
AC -> 14 (Mage Armor); gains Counterspell (1/encounter)
Capstone: Spell Shaping — exclude the player from her AoE
Legendary: once per session, auto-Counterspell

trusted: she admits the institute suppressed her work and shows the coded ledger
bonded: full secret: the whispering Veil glass and her fear about her mentor; her personal quest to find the truth of the Sundering
legendary: her theories begin reshaping how NPCs and factions understand the Veil

voice_notes: alto, quick and precise, warms when a theory excites her, goes flat and careful when frightened

name: Tam
appearance: wiry and quick, frontier leathers, a half-grin that fades fast, always half-turned toward the nearest exit or vantage
speech_style: rapid and enthusiastic in safe moments, breathless in action, suddenly soft and small when vulnerable
mannerisms: bounces on the balls of their feet
goes silent and still when truly scared
backstory_summary: A young Vaelti scout who left the frontier because they couldn't watch the Ashmark creep south one more mile. Courage without a mission, looking for something worth the risk.

free: wilderness tracking, foraging, and reading weather
Ashmark movements and frontier danger signs
where the safe camps and water are on the wild roads
disposition >= friendly: they grew up on the Ashmark frontier and watched it advance
they left without telling their family
they are quietly terrified of being a coward
disposition >= trusted: they have a younger sibling still on the frontier they send money to
they once froze during an Ashmark raid and someone died
the recklessness is penance, not bravery

secrets: they carry a carved token from their sibling and check it is still there constantly
the frozen moment during the raid replays every time combat starts

passives.name: Scout's Instinct
passives.description: Tam cannot be surprised and has advantage on Perception checks in the wilderness. Before encounters, Tam often spots danger first.

passives.name: Flanker
passives.description: When Tam and the player attack the same target in the same phase, Tam's attack has advantage.

actives.name: Reckless Charge
actives.description: Tam charges an enemy, dealing +1d8 damage on hit. Attacks against Tam have advantage until next phase. High-risk, high-reward.

actives.name: Nature's Touch
actives.description: Primal healing learned from frontier life — Tam heals the player or themselves for 1d8 + WIS mod HP. An emergency patch, not a primary heal.

reactions.name: Sidestep
reactions.description: When targeted by a melee attack, Tam moves 5 ft. If out of reach, the attack misses. Tam is hard to pin down.

progression.gains: Nature's Touch unlocks
Reckless Charge damage -> +2d8
+1 to a primary attribute; HP scales via the player HP formula
Capstone: Extra Attack
Legendary: once per session, attack all enemies in range

trusted: they admit they left home without a word and fear they are a coward
bonded: full secret: the frozen moment during the raid and the sibling they support; their personal quest to make it right
legendary: their courage becomes steadiness; NPCs on the frontier know their name

voice_notes: bright tenor, fast and breathless, drops to a near-whisper in vulnerable moments

name: Sable
appearance: a sleek dark fox edged in shifting shadow, eyes that catch impossible light, moves without sound
speech_style: non-verbal — communicates entirely through chirps, growls, posture, and movement; the DM narrates her behavior in a softer, more intimate register
mannerisms: ears flatten and hackles rise near corruption
presses against the player when they are hurt
backstory_summary: A semi-sentient shadow-fox bonded to ambient arcane energy. She doesn't speak. Where she came from and why she chose the player is a mystery she answers only in fragments, through behavior the player learns to read.

free: the presence of Hollow corruption within range — felt, not reasoned
the player's location and emotional state at all times
what hides in the dark ahead
disposition >= friendly: she reacts to certain places as if she remembers them
she guards specific objects without explanation
disposition >= trusted: she leads the player toward something she seems to have been searching for
her shadow-bond strengthens near pre-Sundering Veil sites

secrets: she is drawn to a specific Veil site she has never reached
she was bonded to someone before the player — and grieves them

attacks.name: Bite
attacks.special: If Sable has advantage: +1d4 psychic (shadow-fox arcane nature)
attacks.scaling: psychic +1d6 at L5, +1d8 at L11, +1d10 at L15

passives.name: Veil Sense
passives.description: Sable detects Hollow corruption within 120 ft automatically. The DM narrates her reaction — ears flatten, hackles rise, low growl. The player's early warning system.

passives.name: Shadow Meld
passives.description: In dim light or darkness, Sable is effectively invisible and can scout ahead undetected. The DM narrates what she finds.
passives.scaling: At L10: works in any lighting

passives.name: Pack Bond
passives.description: Sable always knows the player's location and emotional state. If the player is Fallen, she stays at their side; creatures within 5 ft attack the Fallen player at disadvantage.

actives.name: Alarm
actives.description: Sable watches an area and alerts the player with a sharp bark if anything approaches. Eliminates surprise during rest.

actives.name: Distraction
actives.description: Sable darts into the fray, drawing one enemy's attention — it must attack her instead of the player next phase (WIS save negates). Risky given her low HP.

progression.gains: Veil Sense range -> 200 ft
+1 to a primary attribute; HP scales via the player HP formula
Shadow Meld works in any lighting (not just dim)
Capstone: Bite psychic damage scales to +1d10
Legendary: once per session, reveal all hidden creatures and objects within 300 ft

trusted: she begins leading the player toward the place she has been searching for
bonded: full reveal: the Veil site she is drawn to and the bond she grieves; her wordless personal quest
legendary: her presence becomes a story force — even hostile creatures hesitate near her

voice_notes: no TTS speech — the DM narrates her vocalizations from her sound_palette in voice_registry.json; her silence is loud
